baseline.methodology: Bayesian posterior: intercept + controls + Fourier seasonality

ai_insights.executive_summary: The Bayesian Marketing Mix Model (R² = 97.5%) analyzed 104 weeks of ShopNova data across 6 paid channels. All model parameters (adstock decay, saturation rates, channel betas) were learned from data via MCMC sampling with 4x1000 posterior draws — no hardcoded values. Current budget $175,000/week generates ~$1,010,810/week in media-attributable revenue. Optimization yields +12.6% lift (90% CI: [+7.8%, +24.4%], P(positive) = 100%).

key_findings.title: Email: Highest ROI (26.0x)
key_findings.detail: ROI 25.99x. Learned adstock decay = 0.129. Saturation at 69%.

key_findings.title: Display: Most saturated (84%)
key_findings.detail: Highest diminishing returns. Learned saturation lambda = 3.003.

key_findings.title: Uncertainty-aware optimization
key_findings.detail: The Bayesian posterior quantifies confidence: 100% probability that optimization outperforms current allocation. Lift range: +7.8% to +24.4%.
key_findings.action: Use credible intervals to set risk-adjusted budgets.

key_findings.title: Organic + Direct = 9% of revenue
key_findings.detail: Healthy brand equity provides a stable revenue baseline. Paid media drives incremental growth on top.
key_findings.action: Maintain SEO investment. Track brand search volume as health indicator.

ai_insights.optimization_narrative: The optimizer equalizes marginal returns across channels (learned from posterior means) while enforcing minimum spend floors. Key moves: Paid Search down $2,374; Paid Social up $10,811; Display down $7,750; Email up $9,968; TV / OTT down $16,500; Affiliate up $5,846. Net: +12.6% lift at same total spend.
ai_insights.methodology: Bayesian MMM via PyMC-Marketing v0.18+. GeometricAdstock(l_max=10, normalize=True) + LogisticSaturation. MCMC: 4 chains x 1500 tune + 1000 draws, target_accept=0.9. Fit time: 150s. R² = 0.9747. Priors: adstock_alpha ~ Beta(1,3), saturation_lam ~ Gamma(3,1), saturation_beta ~ HalfNormal(sigma=spend_share). Controls: Nov/Dec indicators, linear trend, 2-mode Fourier seasonality.
